title: Cunningham Cabin Floor Plan, Grand Teton NP (HABS WYO-25, sheet 2)
creator: Historic American Buildings Survey (HABS WYO-25), delineated by Charles B. Goldy Jr.
rightsholder_or_source: Library of Congress HABS, via Wikimedia Commons (public domain US Government work)
research_notes: High-resolution PNG (9634x7584) hosted on Wikimedia Commons; original is LoC HABS sheet 2 of 5 (wy0038). Plan only (no elevations on this sheet).
research_features: Tiny/simple vernacular test case with an unusual configuration: a classic DOG-TROT cabin = Living Room and Blacksmith Shop (with forge and anvil noted) flanking an open central BREEZEWAY, total footprint only 41'-5" x 15'-3". Full running dimension chains on all sides, log-wall hatching, scale bars in BOTH feet and meters, and a north arrow. Great for thick-log-wall, open-passage, and dual-unit-measurement handling.
feature_highlights: dog-trot plan: two log pens flanking an open central breezeway
thick hewn/round log walls with timber hatching
blacksmith-shop pen with forge, anvil, and work bench callouts
complete running dimension chains on all four sides
north arrow plus dual feet-and-meter graphic scale bars
note flagging a log wall as a later addition
supported_examples: straight log walls with wall thickness
two rectangular rooms (Living Room, Blacksmith Shop)
simple door openings as gaps in the log walls
linear running dimensions in imperial units
dual imperial and metric scale bars for underlay calibration
roadmap_examples: wall construction profiles for thick log walls and poché
site/textual metadata for the forge and work bench fixtures

gap_features.why: the central BREEZEWAY is a roofed-but-open dog-trot passage that must be drawable as a space without being a fully wall-enclosed room
the sheet carries a north arrow and dual feet/meter graphic scale bars as plan annotations
